untranslatable_reason: WML momentum factor requires cross-sectional ranking of past returns across a portfolio universe (Oct 1993 - Sep 2019 lookback on India market). This is a factor construction methodology that cannot be expressed as point-in-time indicator conditions on a single security. Requires: (1) historical return rankings across multiple securities, (2) portfolio formation logic, (3) cross-sectional sorting. The allowed indicator set lacks portfolio-level constructs, ranking operators, and multi-security aggregation functions needed to implement this signal.
translation_notes: WML (Winners Minus Losers) is a market-neutral long/short factor strategy based on momentum. It involves forming decile portfolios ranked by past returns and taking long positions in high-momentum stocks while shorting low-momentum stocks. Monthly rebalancing is specified. This requires portfolio-construction logic outside the scope of single-security technical indicator rules. A proper implementation would need a custom portfolio engine, not rule-based entry/exit conditions.
